stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains, including transformers and text-generation.
The mention of 'llama' and 'Meta' indicates relevance to major large language model developments.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a generative AI model.
The associated tags (transformers, safetensors) point to its implementation within the standard ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Google must let publishers opt out of AI Search features, rules UK
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: UK regulator (CMA) has mandated that Google must allow publishers to opt out of its AI Search features.
This ruling directly impacts how content from online publishers is used in features like AI Overviews.
The decision represents a significant regulatory shift, giving content owners more control over AI consumption of their material.

stories.title: Microsoft’s next-gen quantum chip cuts timeline to useful quantum computing
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Microsoft is advancing its quantum computing efforts with the announcement of Majorana 2.
Majorana 2 is the next-generation topological quantum chip, succeeding the initial Majorana 1 processor.
The development aims to significantly cut the timeline toward achieving useful quantum computing capabilities.

stories.title: Humanoid-GPT: Scaling Data and Structure for Zero-Shot Motion Tracking
stories.author: Li Yi
bullet_points: Introduction of Humanoid-GPT, a GPT-style Transformer designed for whole-body motion tracking in humanoids.
The model is trained on a massive, unified 2B-frame motion corpus, addressing limitations of prior shallow trackers.
Scaling data and model capacity allows Humanoid-GPT to track highly dynamic behaviors with improved generalization.

stories.title: 1-Click GitHub Token Stealing via a VSCode Bug
stories.author: ammar2
bullet_points: A critical security vulnerability was discovered in VSCode.
The bug allows for the theft of GitHub tokens with a single click.
This represents a significant security risk impacting developers using VSCode and GitHub.

stories.title: Full Disclosure: 1-Click GitHub Token Stealing via a VSCode Bug
stories.author: blog.ammaraskar.com by ammar2
bullet_points: A critical vulnerability exists in VSCode that allows for one-click GitHub token stealing.
The exploit targets GitHub tokens, representing a high-severity security risk for developers.
The disclosure highlights the importance of security auditing in popular developer tools like VSCode.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a specific model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, trending on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It relates to core NLP/ML technologies, specifically text-generation pipelines.
Associated tags indicate the use of standard ML frameworks like transformers and PyTorch.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects users to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all AI processing runs entirely on the user's local device, emphasizing privacy.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users through a partnership with ROOST.
The models are openly available under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open-source safety tooling.

stories.title: Trump signs executive order to review AI models before they’re released
stories.author: Lauren Feiner
bullet_points: President Trump issued an executive order mandating a 'voluntary framework' for AI companies.
The framework requires companies to share frontier AI models with the federal government pre-release.
The stated goals are to promote secure innovation and bolster cybersecurity for critical infrastructure.

stories.title: Microsoft's Project Solara is an Android OS designed for agents instead of apps
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Microsoft is developing Project Solara, a new Android OS.
The OS is specifically designed to support AI agents rather than traditional mobile applications.
This signals a potential industry shift toward agent-centric computing over app-centric models.

stories.title: Amazon-owned Ring should pay Americans for scanning their faces, lawsuit says
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Ring cameras, owned by Amazon, are reportedly using AI to scan and identify faces of guests and passersby.
A lawsuit alleges that Ring is collecting biometric data from Americans without proper compensation or consent.
The story highlights growing legal and ethical concerns regarding the deployment of facial recognition technology in consumer smart home devices.

stories.title: Android phones will soon be able to detect spoofed calls and impersonation scams
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Android phones are receiving new features to detect spoofed calls and impersonation scams.
The update leverages Google's AI capabilities for enhanced security.
The June Android feature drop also includes updates to AirDrop and other features.

stories.title: Language Models Need Sleep: Learning to Self-Modify and Consolidate Memories
stories.author: Vahab Mirrokni
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation in current LLMs: the inability to continually learn and transfer temporal in-context knowledge to long-term parameters.
Proposes a novel approach inspired by human learning, suggesting LLMs require a mechanism analogous to 'sleep' for memory consolidation.
Focuses on enabling self-modification capabilities within Large Language Models to improve long-term learning.

stories.title: Agentic Chain-of-Thought Steering for Efficient and Controllable LLM Reasoning
stories.author: Julian McAuley
bullet_points: Introduces Agentic Chain-of-Thought Steering (ACTS) to improve LLM reasoning efficiency and controllability.
Addresses the inefficiency and lack of inference-time control in standard extended chain-of-thought methods.
Frames reasoning steering as a Markov decision process managed by a controller agent.

stories.title: MAI-Code-1-Flash
stories.author: EvanZhouDev
bullet_points: Microsoft is launching seven new MAI models, including MAI-Code-1-Flash.
The announcement points to advancements in AI models, with specific documentation available.
This represents a significant update in Microsoft's AI model offerings.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released as an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is its local-first design, allowing AI to run on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API services.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated, can read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives like gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: AI has a water problem. Google thinks it has a fix
stories.author: Lauren Feiner
bullet_points: Google is addressing environmental concerns regarding AI data center expansion.
The company is focusing on minimizing water usage impact by increasing water availability for local communities.
Google has published five commitments detailing its new water use strategy.

stories.title: I held the next-gen handheld
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: Intel is facing significant industry headwinds, including layoffs and competitive losses to AMD and Apple/Qualcomm.
The Panther Lake laptop chip, manufactured on the 18A process, demonstrated excellent performance.
The article highlights the positive reception of a next-generation handheld device utilizing this new Intel chip.

stories.title: Microsoft plans Linux tools and an RTX Spark desktop for Windows developers
stories.author: Andrew Cunningham
bullet_points: Microsoft is expanding Linux support for Windows developers with new tools.
The announcement includes plans for a specialized 'RTX Spark' desktop aimed at developers.
The news highlights several software advancements presented at Microsoft Build.

stories.title: Mathematicians warn of AI threats to profession as industry encroaches
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Mathematicians have issued warnings regarding the growing influence of the tech industry on their profession.
The International Mathematical Union (IMU) has formally endorsed these concerns.
The core issue revolves around the encroachment of industry practices and AI into mathematical domains.

stories.title: Imaginative Perception Tokens Enhance Spatial Reasoning in Multimodal Language Models
stories.author: Ranjay Krishna
bullet_points: The research addresses a key limitation in Vision Language Models (VLMs): poor spatial reasoning when information is not directly visible.
The core contribution is the introduction of Imaginative Perception Tokens (IPT), which serve as intermediate perceptual representations.
IPTs aim to enable VLMs to perform 'imaginative perception,' such as inferring unseen viewpoints or tracing occluded paths.

stories.title: Quantifying Faithful Confidence Expression in Large Reasoning Models
stories.author: Arman Cohan
bullet_points: The paper addresses the critical issue of 'faithful calibration' (FC) in Large Reasoning Models (LRMs).
FC refers to the alignment between a model's actual uncertainty and the confidence it linguistically expresses.
Reliable uncertainty communication is vital for building trust in LLMs, especially given how users interpret LRM reasoning traces.

stories.title: QUBRIC: Co-Designing Queries and Rubrics for RL Beyond Verifiable Rewards
stories.author: Chao Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces QUBRIC, a framework designed to advance Reinforcement Learning (RL) beyond reliance on strictly verifiable rewards.
Addresses a key limitation in existing rubric-based RL methods: the assumption of a fixed query distribution.
Proposes co-designing queries and rubrics to overcome the bottleneck where query structure constrains rubric quality.

stories.title: AlignAtt4LLM: Fast AlignAtt for Decoder-Only LLMs at IWSLT 2026 Simultaneous Speech Translation Task
stories.author: Dominik Macháček
bullet_points: Introduces AlignAtt4LLM, a new system for simultaneous speech translation (English to German, Italian, Chinese) designed for IWSLT 2026.
It utilizes a synchronous cascade architecture, combining Qwen3-ASR for incremental transcription and Gemma-4 E4B-it for translation.
A key novelty is its first application of AlignAtt to a decoder-only LLM, adapting the technique without traditional encoder-decoder cross-attention.

stories.title: Self-Refining Agentic Reinforcement Learning for Vision-Conditioned UAV Navigation
stories.author: Dzmitry Tsetserukou
bullet_points: Introduces AgenticRL, a framework designed to enhance autonomy in reinforcement learning for robotics.
Addresses the limitations of traditional RL, specifically the heavy reliance on manual reward function design and fine-tuning.
Focuses on applying this self-refining approach to complex tasks like vision-conditioned navigation for UAVs (drones).

stories.title: Using Reward Uncertainty to Induce Diverse Behaviour in Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Mark Rowland
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in classical Reinforcement Learning (RL) where policies tend to be deterministic, which is insufficient for modern tasks requiring diversity (e.g., LLM fine-tuning).
It proposes using 'Reward Uncertainty' as a novel mechanism to naturally induce diverse behavior in RL agents.
The proposed method aims to overcome limitations of existing diversity techniques, such as entropy regularization, which often involve fragile performance trade-offs.

stories.title: AI outperforms law professors in Stanford Law study
stories.author: berlianta
bullet_points: AI systems demonstrated performance surpassing human law professors in a Stanford Law study.
The research suggests advanced AI capabilities are encroaching on traditionally human, knowledge-intensive fields like law.
The study, linked from Stanford Law, provides empirical data on AI's proficiency in legal reasoning or analysis.
